questions.question: Why does grinding chalk not change its chemical nature?
questions.answer: Because the particles remain the same substance even when broken into smaller pieces.

questions.question: Why does sugar dissolve in water without increasing the volume much?
questions.answer: Sugar particles occupy spaces between water particles.

questions.question: What property of matter explains diffusion?
questions.answer: Continuous motion of particles.

questions.question: Why do solids resist compression?
questions.answer: Because their particles are tightly packed with very little space between them.

questions.question: Why can liquids change shape but maintain volume?
questions.answer: Because liquid particles can move but remain close together.

questions.question: Why do gases spread rapidly in all directions?
questions.answer: Because gas particles move freely with very weak attraction between them.

questions.question: Why are gases highly compressible compared to liquids?
questions.answer: Because gases contain large empty spaces between particles.

questions.question: What does the syringe compression experiment prove about gases?
questions.answer: It proves that gases have large spaces between particles.

questions.question: Why does diffusion occur faster in hot conditions?
questions.answer: Higher temperature increases particle kinetic energy and movement.

questions.question: Why does incense smoke spread throughout a room?
questions.answer: Because gas particles diffuse rapidly through air.

questions.question: Why does potassium permanganate spread uniformly in water?
questions.answer: Because particles in liquids are constantly moving and diffusing.

questions.question: What happens to particle attraction when a solid melts?
questions.answer: Interparticle attraction becomes weaker.

questions.question: Why is melting considered a physical change?
questions.answer: Because only the physical state changes while the substance remains the same.

questions.question: Why does boiling occur throughout the liquid?
questions.answer: Because particles gain enough energy to escape as gas throughout the liquid.

questions.question: Why does evaporation occur only at the surface of liquids?
questions.answer: Because only surface particles gain enough energy to escape into vapor.

questions.question: Why does wind increase evaporation rate?
questions.answer: Wind removes vapor particles from the surface, allowing more evaporation.

questions.question: Why does increasing surface area increase evaporation?
questions.answer: More particles are exposed to escape into vapor.

questions.question: Why do gases have very low density?
questions.answer: Because particles are widely spaced.

questions.question: What happens to interparticle spacing when matter changes from solid to gas?
questions.answer: Particle spacing increases greatly.

questions.question: Why are liquids and gases called fluids?
questions.answer: Because their particles can move freely allowing them to flow.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the highest particle kinetic energy?
questions.answer: Gases.

questions.question: Why do solids generally have high density?
questions.answer: Because particles are tightly packed with minimal space between them.

questions.question: Why do gas particles exert pressure on container walls?
questions.answer: Because they continuously collide with the walls of the container.

questions.question: Why does diffusion not occur easily in solids?
questions.answer: Because solid particles are tightly packed and cannot move freely.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the particle model of matter?
questions.answer: Matter consists of tiny particles that have spaces between them and are in constant motion.
